summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture of a stellar late‑1979 show, highlighted by a smoking Friend of the Devil and a uniquely stacked second set (Scarlet > Fire, Terrapin, Playing in the Band, Passenger).
review: The recording is frequently praised as one of the cleanest tape‑master SBDs ever released – crisp, well‑separated and virtually boom‑free, with only a brief buzz on the opening Alabama. Musically the show shines with standout performances of Peggy O., a short‑form Cassidy, Friend of the Devil and an unforgettable second set that uniquely strings together Scarlet > Fire, Terrapin, Playing in the Band and Passenger, despite a minor vocal slip on El Paso.

ai_rating.rationale: The overwhelming majority of reviews (6 out of 7) give 5‑star praise for both the soundboard quality and the musical performance; only one review is negative about setlist uniqueness, yielding an average rating around 4.1‑4.3.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clean, separated soundstage with great definition and little boominess; a brief buzz on the opening Alabama but otherwise crystal‑clear. Reviewers note the classic ‘clinky’ late‑70s board tone and an immersive Phil Zone.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The first set builds energy with bright playing and a strong Alabama kickoff, loses a little steam after the drum break but regains momentum. The second set is uniquely historic – it’s the only known show to feature all five of Scarlet > Fire, Terrapin, Playing in the Band, Passenger plus the earlier songs together.

band_member_comments.Jerry: No specific remarks, but the mix lets his guitar sit cleanly within the overall bright soundstage.
band_member_comments.Phil: Repeatedly praised; his bass is described as ‘the Phil Zone’ and as ‘attacking Alabama Getaway’, indicating a powerful, front‑and‑center performance.
band_member_comments.Bob: One reviewer notes Bob Weir missed vocals on El Paso, pointing to a brief vocal lapse.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland’s early contributions are highlighted as bright, meandering and adding fresh texture to the set.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drums drive the early energy (‘sticks counting off’) though one comment mentions a slight loss of steam after the drum break.
